CheckTitle: Repository rulesets dismiss stale reviews when new commits are pushed
Description: GitHub repository has an active ruleset configured to dismiss stale pull request approvals when new commits are pushed. Without this, a previously approved PR could be merged after new code is added without re-review.
Risk: If stale reviews are not dismissed on push, a malicious actor could get a PR approved and then push additional changes without requiring a fresh review, potentially introducing unauthorized code.

Code.Other:
1. In GitHub, open the repository and go to Settings > Rules > Rulesets
2. Create or edit a ruleset targeting your default branch
3. Under 'Require a pull request before merging', enable 'Dismiss stale pull request approvals when new commits are pushed'
4. Click Save changes

Recommendation.Text: Create or update a GitHub Repository Ruleset targeting the default branch and enable 'Dismiss stale pull request approvals when new commits are pushed'. Migrate from legacy branch protection rules to rulesets for automated verification.

Notes: This check prioritises GitHub Repository Rulesets over legacy branch protection rules. Repositories still using legacy rules receive MANUAL status with migration guidance. Archived repositories are skipped.
